title: Huhtamaki Oyj (HOYFF) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Highlights: Record Margins and Dividend Growth ...
content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Adjusted EBIT Margin: Improved above 10% for both the quarter and full year, reaching record highs. Adjusted EPS: Maintained at 2.48, matching last year's all-time high. Dividend Proposal: Increase to EUR 1.14 per share, pending AGM approval. Net Sales: EUR 981 million for the quarter, impacted by a 6% currency headwind. Full Year Currency Impact: 3.2% headwind, resulting in a EUR 125 million impact. Adjusted EBIT: EUR 103 million for the quarter; EUR 405 million for the full year. Free Cash Flow: EUR 311 million, up EUR 95 million from last year. Foodservice Segment Margin: 9.8% for the quarter; 9.3% for the full year. North America Segment Margin: 12.1% for the quarter; RONA at 15% for the full year. Flexibles Packaging Margin: 9.2% EBIT margin for the full year, an all-time high. Fiber Packaging Segment Margin: 15.9% for the quarter; 13.3% for the full year. Net Debt to EBITDA: Reduced to 1.9, down from 3.1 in 2021. Average Interest Rate on Gross Debt: Approximately 4%. Dividend Payout Ratio: Proposed at 46% with the EUR 1.14 dividend.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 4 Warning Sign with HOYFF. High Yield Dividend Stocks in Gurus' Portfolio This Powerful Chart Made Peter Lynch 29% A Year For 13 Years How to calculate the intrinsic value of a stock? Is HOYFF fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

Release Date: February 13, 2026

For the complete transcript of the earnings call, please refer to the full earnings call transcript.

Positive Points

Huhtamaki Oyj (HOYFF) achieved an adjusted EBIT margin above 10% for the second time in its history, reaching record highs both in the quarter and the full year. The company reported an all-time high adjusted EPS of 2.48, matching last year's record level. The Board of Directors proposed a dividend increase to EUR 1.14 per share, marking the 17th consecutive year of dividend growth. Huhtamaki Oyj (HOYFF) demonstrated strong cash flow generation, with a focus on capital discipline leading to a significant increase in free cash flow. The Flexibles segment showed significant margin improvement and achieved its best-ever performance with a 9.2% EBIT margin.

Negative Points

The company faced a challenging market environment with geopolitical issues, tariffs, and significant FX movements impacting financial results. Comparable net sales were down 1.3% for the full year, with a notable impact from currency fluctuations. The Foodservice segment experienced a soft top-line performance, particularly in Western Europe and the UK, leading to a decline in comparable sales. North America faced pricing pressure due to low consumer confidence, impacting overall comparable growth despite volume increases. Severe weather conditions in North America in January led to temporary closures of sites, which is expected to impact business results.

Story Continues

Q & A Highlights

Q: Can you elaborate on the soft growth in food service packaging, particularly in Western markets and the UK? A: The market was primarily soft, with some customers attempting promotions to boost traffic, but most did not succeed. This softness was particularly evident in Western Europe and the UK, impacting our performance. However, we remain committed to supporting our customers as they continue their promotional efforts. - Ralf Wunderlich, CEO

Q: Are you experiencing increased competition in North America? A: We have not observed a significant increase in competition beyond what was previously reported. With consumer confidence low, there is pressure on pricing, but we have chosen to maintain our market presence, resulting in volume growth despite pricing challenges. - Ralf Wunderlich, CEO

Q: What is the outlook for comparable sales in the flexible packaging and food service segments for H1? A: We do not provide specific volume outlooks, but the focus remains on profitability and product mix improvements, particularly in flexible packaging. The weather in North America has impacted operations, but we are assessing the situation. - Ralf Wunderlich, CEO

Q: Are you seeing any benefits from tariffs in North America? A: The tariff situation is complex, but we have seen some shifts from Chinese imports to our products, particularly in retail. However, as we produce and sell domestically, the overall impact of tariffs on our business is not significant. - Ralf Wunderlich, CEO

Q: How should we think about free cash flow in 2026, considering the negative working capital impact in 2025? A: We remain focused on capital discipline, with no significant changes expected in CapEx. We are managing working capital effectively, and there is no reason to expect a different approach in 2026. - Ralf Wunderlich, CEO

Q: How are raw materials and costs developing, particularly in food service and North America? A: Most of our contracts have automatic pass-through clauses, so raw material price changes primarily affect the top line rather than the bottom line. We do not anticipate significant impacts from raw material costs in 2026. - Ralf Wunderlich, CEO

Q: Is there potential for organic growth in Q2 due to past investments and easier comps? A: We are optimistic about the positive impacts of past investments and hope for improved consumer confidence. The investments made in 2025 and prior years should start showing positive results. - Ralf Wunderlich, CEO

Q: Are there any updates on your operations in India? A: The turnaround in India is outperforming expectations, with significant improvements in performance. We are very pleased with the progress in India and Turkey. - Ralf Wunderlich, CEO

For the complete transcript of the earnings call, please refer to the full earnings call transcript.

title: 3 European Dividend Stocks Offering Up To 5.5% Yield
content:
As optimism about the eurozone economy bolsters investor sentiment, European markets have shown resilience amid recent volatility, with major indexes like Germany's DAX and France's CAC 40 posting gains. In this environment, dividend stocks can offer a compelling option for investors seeking steady income streams; these stocks are often valued for their ability to provide returns even when market conditions fluctuate.

Top 10 Dividend Stocks In Europe

Click here to see the full list of 193 stocks from our Top European Dividend Stocks screener.

Here we highlight a subset of our preferred stocks from the screener.

Eni

Simply Wall St Dividend Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Eni S.p.A. is an integrated energy company with operations across Italy, the EU, the US, Asia, Africa, and globally, with a market cap of approximately €54.20 billion.

Operations: Eni S.p.A.'s revenue is primarily derived from its Exploration & Production segment (€51.65 billion), Global Gas & LNG Portfolio and Power (€18.72 billion), and Refining and Chemicals (€18.70 billion).

Dividend Yield: 5.6%

Eni offers a compelling dividend yield of 5.6%, placing it in the top 25% of Italian dividend payers. Its dividends are well-covered by earnings, with a payout ratio of 29.2%, and supported by cash flows at a cash payout ratio of 77.2%. However, its dividend history has been volatile over the past decade, lacking consistent growth or stability. The recent €993.42 million fixed-income offering may impact future financial flexibility and dividend sustainability.

Click to explore a detailed breakdown of our findings in Eni's dividend report. In light of our recent valuation report, it seems possible that Eni is trading behind its estimated value.BIT:ENI Dividend History as at Feb 2026

Huhtamäki Oyj

Simply Wall St Dividend Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: Huhtamäki Oyj is a global provider of packaging solutions with operations in countries including the United States, Germany, and India, and has a market cap of €3.26 billion.

Operations: Huhtamäki Oyj's revenue segments include North America (€1.43 billion), Fiber Packaging (€381 million), Flexible Packaging (€1.28 billion), and Foodservice Packaging (€962.5 million).

Story Continues

Dividend Yield: 3.5%

Huhtamäki Oyj provides a stable dividend yield of 3.54%, though it falls short of the Finnish market's top 25% dividend payers. Its dividends have shown consistent growth and stability over the past decade, supported by a payout ratio of 57.7% and cash flow coverage at 61.9%. Despite trading significantly below its estimated fair value, Huhtamäki's high debt levels could pose risks to future dividend sustainability. Recent executive changes emphasize a focus on sustainability and corporate affairs leadership.

Take a closer look at Huhtamäki Oyj's potential here in our dividend report. Insights from our recent valuation report point to the potential undervaluation of Huhtamäki Oyj shares in the market.HLSE:HUH1V Dividend History as at Feb 2026

SAF-Holland

Simply Wall St Dividend Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: SAF-Holland SE manufactures and sells chassis-related assemblies and components for trailers, trucks, semi-trailers, and buses with a market cap of €824.38 million.

Operations: SAF-Holland SE generates its revenue from three main geographical segments: €663.11 million from the Americas, €208.62 million from Asia/Pacific (APAC)/China/India, and €861.36 million from Europe, the Middle East, and Africa (EMEA).

Dividend Yield: 4.6%

SAF-Holland's dividend yield of 4.63% ranks in the top 25% of German dividend payers, though its payout history has been volatile over the past decade. Despite this instability, dividends are well-covered by earnings and cash flows with a payout ratio of 68.9% and cash payout at 35.4%. The company trades at a discount to its estimated fair value but faces challenges with high debt levels and revised sales guidance for fiscal year 2025.

title: Riikka Tieaho appointed Executive Vice President, Sustainability, Corporate Affairs & Legal, and General Counsel
content:
Huhtamäki Oyj

Riikka TieahoPhoto·GlobeNewswire Inc.

HUHTAMÄKI OYJ STOCK EXCHANGE RELEASE 30.01.2026 AT 10:00 (EET)

Riikka Tieaho appointed Executive Vice President, Sustainability, Corporate Affairs & Legal​, and General Counsel

Huhtamaki has appointed Riikka Tieaho (50) as Executive Vice President, Sustainability, Corporate Affairs & Legal, and General Counsel. Riikka will be a member of the Global Executive Team, reporting to President and CEO Ralf K. Wunderlich. She will start in her role no later than June 1, 2026 and will be based in Espoo, Finland.

Riikka joins Huhtamaki from Wolt, where she has served as General Counsel since 2020, and brings 20 years of legal and leadership experience from Nokia, a global publicly listed company.

Aino Kyytsönen has been appointed from within the company to ensure continuity as Interim Senior Vice President, Sustainability, Corporate Affairs & Legal, and General Counsel until Riikka joins Huhtamaki.

“We are pleased to welcome Riikka to Huhtamaki and to our Global Executive Team. Her strong track record leading legal and compliance functions in global, high-growth environments will be instrumental as we continue to strengthen sustainability and governance at the core of our operations while accelerating profitable growth,” says Ralf K. Wunderlich, President and CEO of Huhtamaki.

“I am delighted to join Huhtamaki at a time when sustainability, strong governance, and responsible growth are at the core of the company’s strategy. Huhtamaki's strong heritage, global operating environment, and clear ambition to be a leader in sustainable packaging solutions strongly resonate with me. I look forward to working closely with the Global Executive Team to further strengthen the company’s sustainability, corporate affairs, and legal capabilities, and to contributing to Huhtamaki’s journey toward becoming the first choice in sustainable packaging solutions,” says Riikka Tieaho.

Following the above change, the members of Huhtamaki’s Global Executive Team are: ​
Ralf K. Wunderlich (Chair), President and CEO; 
Fredrik Davidsson, President, Foodservice Packaging;
Sara Engber, President, Fiber Packaging; 
Thomas Geust, Chief Financial Officer; 
Axel Glade, President, Flexible Packaging; 
Katariina Kravi, Executive Vice President, Human Resources, Safety and Communications;
Ann O’Hara, President, North America;
Riikka Tieaho, Executive Vice President, Sustainability, Corporate Affairs and Legal (latest June 1, 2026); and 
Changsheng Wu, Executive Vice President, Procurement.

For further information, please contact:
Ralf K. Wunderlich, President & CEO, tel. +358 10 686 7058

Story Continues

HUHTAMÄKI OYJ
Global Communications

About Huhtamaki
Huhtamaki is a leading global provider of sustainable packaging solutions for consumers around the world. Our innovative products protect on-the-go and on-the-shelf food and beverages, and personal care products, ensuring hygiene and safety, driving accessibility and affordability, and helping prevent food waste. We embed sustainability in everything we do.

Huhtamaki has over 100 years of history and a strong Nordic heritage. Our around 18 000 professionals are operating in 36 countries and 102 locations around the world. Our values are Care Dare Deliver. In 2024 Huhtamaki’s net sales totaled EUR 4.1 billion. Huhtamäki Oyj is listed on the Nasdaq Helsinki and the head office is in Espoo, Finland. Find out more at www.huhtamaki.com.

Attachments

title: Huhtamaki publishes 2025 results on February 13, 2026
content:
Huhtamäki Oyj

HUHTAMAKI PRESS RELEASE 22.1.2026 AT 09:00
Huhtamaki publishes 2025 results on February 13, 2026

Huhtamäki Oyj will publish its 2025 results on Friday, February 13, 2026, at approximately 8:30 (EET). The release and related results presentation material will be available after publishing at https://www.huhtamaki.com/en/investors.

Huhtamaki will arrange a combined audiocast and teleconference on the same day at 9:30. Huhtamaki’s CEO & President Ralf K. Wunderlich and CFO Thomas Geust will present the results. The event will be followed by a Q&A session. The event will be held in English, and it can be followed in real-time.

A link to the audiocast is available at: https://huhtamaki.events.inderes.com/q4-2025
A link to the teleconference is available at: https://events.inderes.com/huhtamaki/q4-2025/dial-in.
Registration is required for the teleconference. After the registration you will be provided with phone numbers and a conference ID to access the conference.

An on-demand replay of the audiocast will be available shortly after the end of the call at https://www.huhtamaki.com/en/investors.

For further information, please contact: 
Kristian Tammela, Vice President, Investor Relations, tel. +358 10 686 7058

title: Huhtamaki raises its climate ambition with updated greenhouse gas reduction targets validated by the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi)
content:
Huhtamäki Oyj

Huhtamaki is raising its climate ambition and adopting more rigorous short-term emission reduction targets. The updated Scope 1 and 2 targets are aligned with the Paris Agreement goal of limiting global warming to 1.5°C.

By 2030, the company aims to reduce Scope 1 and 2* GHG emissions by over 50% and Scope 3 emissions** by 25% compared to 2022 levels. The Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) has approved Huhtamaki’s near-term science-based emissions reduction targets.

In addition, Huhtamaki has committed to setting long-term emissions reduction targets with the SBTi, in line with reaching net-zero by 2050.

“Guided by our vision and our belief that sustainability creates lasting value, we are strengthening our climate targets to make a real, long-term impact. Prioritizing sustainability is more than a requirement; it's a cornerstone of our journey to become the first choice in sustainable packaging solutions. With our packaging innovations and updated ambition, we play an important role in our customers’ sustainability goals and help mitigate transition risks such as carbon pricing. It is essential that we, together with our value chain, collaborate to achieve these science-based goals and drive urgent action to reduce GHG emissions,” says Ralf K. Wunderlich, President and CEO of Huhtamaki.

The company’s climate action plan to achieve the emission reduction targets includes a variety of decarbonization levers aimed at reducing emissions across Scope 1, Scope 2, and Scope 3, such as replacement of fossil fuels, purchasing more energy made with renewables, and prioritizing engagement with suppliers that demonstrate low emissions intensity offering, as well as increasing the renewable and recycled content in products.​ Huhtamaki is also continuously improving operational efficiency, resulting in reduced carbon emissions.

Updated sustainability ambition for increased impact

In late 2025, Huhtamaki updated its overall sustainability ambitions to adapt to the evolving needs of the packaging industry and customers. In addition, the renewed approach strengthens accountability for progress and ensures the setting of technology-relevant targets. Sustainability remains at the core of Huhtamaki’s strategy as the company continues with a holistic ESG-approach, striving to become the first choice in sustainable packaging solutions.

Beyond climate, the environmental focus is on developing packaging designed to be recycled, composted, or reused, made from responsibly sourced materials, minimizing waste, and reducing water consumption.

Story Continues

“Sustainability doesn’t just protect the planet, it drives long-term profitability. We continue to collaborate closely with customers and partners to deliver solutions that are both sustainable, cost-efficient and easy-to-use,” says Ralf K. Wunderlich.

For further information, please contact: Ralf K. Wunderlich CEO & President, Huhtamaki, media@huhtamaki.com

Huhtamaki's SBTi-validated near-term targets:

Scope 1 & 2: Huhtamaki commits to reduce absolute Scope 1 and 2 GHG emissions by 50.44% by 2030, from a 2022 base year.* Scope 3: Huhtamaki also commits to reduce absolute Scope 3 GHG emissions from purchased goods and services, fuel- and energy-related activities, upstream transportation and distribution, waste generated in operations, business travel, and employee commuting by 25.0% within the same timeframe.** Deforestation: Huhtamaki commits to no deforestation across its primary deforestation-linked commodities, with a target date of December 31, 2025.

*The target boundary includes land-related emissions and removals from biogenic feedstocks.

**The target only includes energy and industrial emissions, the FLAG emissions have been excluded. The company will include these emissions in a FLAG target once a pathway for timber and wood fiber is available

About Huhtamaki 
Huhtamaki is a leading global provider of sustainable packaging solutions for consumers around the world. Our innovative products protect on-the-go and on-the-shelf food and beverages, and personal care products, ensuring hygiene and safety, driving accessibility and affordability, and helping prevent food waste. We embed sustainability in everything we do. Huhtamaki has over 100 years of history and a strong Nordic heritage. Our around 18 000 professionals are operating in 36 countries and 102 locations around the world. Our values are Care Dare Deliver. In 2024 Huhtamaki’s net sales totaled EUR 4.1 billion. Huhtamäki Oyj is listed on the Nasdaq Helsinki and the head office is in Espoo, Finland. Find out more at www.huhtamaki.com.
